model.label: Gemini 3 Flash
model.description: Google • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: St. John's enters the tournament as a highly efficient unit, boasting a top-15 defensive ranking and a top-50 offense that led them to a Big East regular-season title. While star guard Kadary Richmond is playing through multiple injuries, his ability to lead the team to significant wins down the stretch, combined with Rick Pitino's extensive tournament experience, gives the Red Storm a clear advantage. Northern Iowa is a formidable 12-seed with a top-30 defense, but their mediocre offensive efficiency will likely struggle against St. John's elite pressure and length.

reasoningStep.title: St. John's vs. Northern Iowa Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: St. John's is favored due to their elite defensive metrics and superior performance in a high-major conference, despite injury concerns for their star player.
evidence: St. John's is ranked 20th in KenPom and 18th in Bart Torvik, with the 12th-ranked adjusted defensive efficiency.
Northern Iowa is ranked 71st in KenPom and 70th in Torvik, with a strong defense (26th) but a weak offense (153rd).
St. John's star Kadary Richmond is playing through injuries but recently led the team to a win over UConn.
Northern Iowa enters with momentum after winning the MVC tournament, but faces a significant talent and efficiency gap.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
